channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A gang leader in Haiti is accused of massacring older people to avenge his son's death
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — A gang leader who controls a key port in Haiti's capital is accused of massacring older people and Vodou religious leaders in his community to avenge his son's death, according to the government and human rights organizations that estimate more than 100 killed.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Israel advances on a Syrian buffer zone, it sees peril and opportunity
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The dramatic downfall of Syrian President Bashar Assad presents possible danger, and an opening, for neighboring Israel. After fighting wars on multiple fronts for months, Israel is now concerned that unrest in Syria could spill over into its territory.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour ends by shattering own record, grossing an estimated $2.2B, Pollstar says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour brought in $2.2 billion in its nearly two-year run, making it the highest-grossing tour of all time for a second year in a row, Pollstar estimates from data collected across 149 shows.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel deploys paratroopers to Syria in 'defense activities' after fall of Assad
item.description: Israeli forces released footage of their deployment into Syria following the fall of Bashar Assad's regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy open to Western troops providing security for end to war in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Monday that he's open to the potential deployment of Western troops in Ukraine to guarantee the country’s security as part of a broad effort to end the almost three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize winner urges Putin to understand the destructiveness of nuclear weapons
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Terumi Tanaka, a survivor of the U.S. atomic bombings of Japan and the representative of an organization that won this year's Nobel Peace Prize, called on Russian President Vladimir Putin on Monday to stop making nuclear threats.
item.dc:creator: FANNY BRODERSEN and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Syrian community in Chicago celebrates end of Assad regime
item.description: It was a sea of green flags on Michigan Avenue Sunday as hundreds of Chicagoans celebrated the end of the regime in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian government still functioning: Prime minister
item.description: Syria’s prime minister said the country’s government is still functioning and most Cabinet ministers are still working, even after rebels entered Damascus and President Bashar Assad fled the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu to testify in corruption trial amid multiple conflicts
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will begin his witness testimony Tuesday for his ongoing trial on corruption and fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fall of Syria's Bashar Assad is strategic blow to Iran and Russia, experts say
item.description: Analysts and commentators say the collapse of Syria's Bashar al-Assad's regime is likely to reshape the power dynamics in the Middle East while signaling a critical shift in influence among world powers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes hit Gaza and also target suspected weapons sites in Syria
item.description: Israeli strikes have hit suspected chemical and long-range weapons sites in Syria and the foreign minister says the aim is to keep them from rebels who seized Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Illness delays trial of men charged with cutting down Britain's scenic Sycamore Gap tree
item.description: The trial of two men charged with cutting down the majestic Sycamore Gap tree last year along Hadrian’s Wall in northern England has been postponed until next year.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Assad granted asylum in Russia, arrives in Moscow
item.description: Syrian President Bashar Assad and his family have been granted asylum in Russia and arrived in Moscow on Sunday after the Syrian government fell.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump calls for immediate ceasefire in Ukraine and says a US withdrawal from NATO is possible
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump on Sunday pushed Russian leader Vladimir Putin to act to reach an immediate ceasefire with Ukraine, describing it as part of his active efforts as president-elect to end the war despite being weeks from taking office.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turner says he believes there will be 'assessment as to whether' troops in Syria 'should' stay under Trump
item.description: House Intelligence Committee Chair Mike Turner (R-Ohio) said Sunday that under President-elect Trump, he believes there will “be an assessment as to whether or not” troops currently in Syria “should” stay.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: President-elect Trump early Sunday called for an "immediate ceasefire" in the fighting between Ukraine and Russia following the fall of the Assad government in Syria and after a meeting in Paris with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky. "Assad is gone. He has fled his country.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: US will remain in eastern Syria and seek to prevent IS resurgence, Pentagon says
item.description: Hours after Syrian rebels announced they had toppled Bashar al-Assad's government, the U.S. deputy assistant secretary of defense for the Middle East said the U.S. will remain in eastern Syria to prevent an ISIS resurgence.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against ‘Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: The nun, 57, is also well-known for her service in Milan's run-down districts. In February she was one of the recipients of the "Golden Panettone", an annual Milanese civic award.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump back on the world stage at Notre Dame reopening in Paris
item.description: Zelenskyy said his first face-to-face encounter with Trump since the election was “good and productive,” adding they had agreed to continue working together.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Assad regime in peril as insurgents near Syria's capital: What to know
item.description: Syrian rebels on Saturday battled government forces in an effort to take control of the crucial city of Homs as they advanced toward the country's capital, Damascus -- moves that catapulted President Bashar al-Assad's nearly 30 years of power into peril, Reuters reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: The famed Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris reopens to great fanfare
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Notre Dame is reopening its doors for the first time since a fire in 2019 nearly destroyed Paris' beloved 12th-century cathedral.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump urges US to stay out of Syrian civil war, blaming Obama for failure as Islamists close in on capital
item.description: Amid the rapid offensive of Islamist rebels in their campaign to dislodge Syrian dictator Assad, President-elect Donal Trump has urged non-intervention by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean president apologizes for declaring martial law ahead of impeachment vote
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol apologized for declaring martial law earlier in the week, as he now prepares for a parliamentary vote on his impeachment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Metal detectorist uncovers fragment of Viking sword in the Netherlands
item.description: A 10th-century Viking sword fragment was discovered by a metal detectorist scouring a field in the Netherlands. Further research is being done to find out more about the ancient item.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actress dies after consuming amphibian's venom at spiritual cleansing retreat
item.description: Mexican actress Marcela Alcázar Rodríguez, 33, died after ingesting Amazonian frog venom during a cleansing ritual at a spiritual retreat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Husband leaps onto polar bear that lunged at wife in surprise attack: police
item.description: A man in Canada quickly defended his wife by jumping onto a polar bear that lunged toward her at the couple's home in rural Ontario, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: US 'seriously concerned' by developments in South Korea
item.description: The United States was surprised by South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol’s brief declaration of emergency martial law.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Hungary’s effort to end Holocaust survivor lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday probed the international consequences of allowing a group of Holocaust survivors and their heirs to leverage U.S. courts to seek compensation from Hungary for confiscating their property.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago welcomes eight children from Gaza for medical treatment
item.description: Over the next few months, the children will receive medical treatment at U.S. hospitals, free of cost.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 11 in Lebanon after fire exchanges with Hezbollah that test ceasefire’s limits
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel unleashed its largest wave of airstrikes across Lebanon since agreeing to a ceasefire with Hezbollah last week, killing at least 11 people on Monday after the Lebanese militant group fired a volley of projectiles as a warning over what it said were Israeli truce violations.
item.dc:creator: NATALIE MELTZER and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation
